land office do not agree with his re-
port in the newspapers.


In sending this rebuke the commits
sioner assumes that dispatch pub
lished in Eastern papers yesterday Is
true. This dispatch quoted Mr.. Leach
as saying that he had found greater
land frauds in the state of Washing
ton than had been found in Oregon.


General land office officials say that
Mr.. Leach is talking too much. At
the same time the commissioner de-
clares that there is nothing either in
Mr.. Leach's reports or in other official
reports which have come here to war
rant such a statement as is attribut-
ed to Leach in the newspaper dis
patches.


AS heretofore told in press dis
patches, the interior department is
working on certain cases, but Secre-
tary Hitchcock does not yet claim to
have found any such frauds as were
committed in oregon, and he Is not
ready to make statement of any
kind.
